verses.text: Ephraim 'feedeth on wind,' chasing the east wind and making vain treaties; God has a controversy with Jacob. Empty pursuits. Trust in shifting alliances feeds on nothing.
Ephraim 'feedeth on wind,' chasing the east wind and making vain treaties; God has a controversy with Jacob. Empty pursuits. Trust in shifting alliances feeds on nothing.

Jacob wrestled and 'had power with God'; the LORD is His memorial; 'turn thou to thy God: keep mercy and judgment, and wait... continually.' A father's example. The God who met Jacob calls his sons to return and wait on Him.

Jacob wrestled and 'had power with God'; the LORD is His memorial; 'turn thou to thy God: keep mercy and judgment, and wait... continually.' A father's example. The God who met Jacob calls his sons to return and wait on Him.

Jacob wrestled and 'had power with God'; the LORD is His memorial; 'turn thou to thy God: keep mercy and judgment, and wait... continually.' A father's example. The God who met Jacob calls his sons to return and wait on Him.

Jacob wrestled and 'had power with God'; the LORD is His memorial; 'turn thou to thy God: keep mercy and judgment, and wait... continually.' A father's example. The God who met Jacob calls his sons to return and wait on Him.
Ephraim is a merchant with 'balances of deceit,' boasting, 'I am rich,' that none can find his sin. Dishonest gain. Wealth got by fraud blinds a man to his own guilt.
Ephraim is a merchant with 'balances of deceit,' boasting, 'I am rich,' that none can find his sin. Dishonest gain. Wealth got by fraud blinds a man to his own guilt.
God, the LORD from Egypt, will yet make them dwell in tents again, and has spoken by prophets and parables. Patient teaching. God uses many means to recall His people.
God, the LORD from Egypt, will yet make them dwell in tents again, and has spoken by prophets and parables. Patient teaching. God uses many means to recall His people.
Is there iniquity in Gilead? Jacob fled to Syria and served for a wife and kept sheep. History recalled. The nation's roots are traced to humble, dependent beginnings.
Is there iniquity in Gilead? Jacob fled to Syria and served for a wife and kept sheep. History recalled. The nation's roots are traced to humble, dependent beginnings.
By a prophet God brought Israel from Egypt; yet Ephraim provoked Him bitterly, and his blood shall be on him. Grace forgotten. The people delivered by mercy answer with provocation.
By a prophet God brought Israel from Egypt; yet Ephraim provoked Him bitterly, and his blood shall be on him. Grace forgotten. The people delivered by mercy answer with provocation.
